danger_signals.visual_form: Tetrominoes accumulating toward top of playfield; blocks visually approach the ceiling
danger_signals.meaning: Imminent game over; failure state approaching if player cannot clear lines

danger_signals.visual_form: Red, yellow, and blue virus sprites scattered across playfield
danger_signals.meaning: Threats to clear; unmatched viruses represent active danger/obstacle

danger_signals.visual_form: Colored pill objects accumulating upward toward screen top
danger_signals.meaning: Game over condition approaching; player must eliminate viruses before space runs out

safety_signals.visual_form: Completed horizontal rows flash and disappear; blocks above fall
safety_signals.meaning: Successful action; player is managing space effectively

safety_signals.visual_form: Matched virus+pill combinations vanish from playfield
safety_signals.meaning: Progress toward stage completion; threat removal

safety_signals.visual_form: Large open space at base of play area
safety_signals.meaning: Player has breathing room; no immediate threat

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Viruses in three color variants (red, yellow, blue); single-color and dual-color pills
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Medical/pharmaceutical theme (Dr. character theme); playfield represents diagnostic grid

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Seven tetromino types with distinct shapes and colors

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Few viruses scattered sparsely across lower portion of playfield
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Viruses remain stationary; player has ample space and time to plan moves

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: More viruses populate playfield; increased color variety on screen
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Virus density increases; player must work faster to avoid pills reaching top

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Tetrominoes fall at moderate speed; playfield mostly empty
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Falling blocks descent at standard rate; player has time to rotate and position

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Blocks/pills occupy upper half of playfield; stack visibly approaches ceiling
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Visual warning of imminent loss; sense of urgency; no recovery margin

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: All viruses removed; playfield now empty or minimal objects visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Transition to next stage with increased virus count/difficulty

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Game switches between Tetris and Dr. Mario visual/rule systems; screen updates to reflect active game type
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Players accumulate points across alternating game modes; scoring context shifts per mode

environmental_storytelling.example: Red, yellow, blue viruses establish pharmaceutical/medical diagnostic narrative; pills must match virus colors to 'cure' them
Multiple stages with escalating virus density communicate difficulty ramp and sense of deeper viral infection requiring stronger intervention
Gravity-driven falling tetrominoes create inherent pressure/urgency without explicit narrative; geometry itself communicates challenge
Gridlines and cell-based layout communicate systematic, ordered world where chaos (unmatched blocks/viruses) must be conquered through precision

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Descends continuously toward bottom at consistent speed; rotates per player input; locks when colliding with bottom or other blocks
Remains stationary in playfield; visually persists as obstacle/threat until matched with two or more same-colored pills
Descends under gravity; can be two-colored (one color per half); rotates per player input; stops when colliding with viruses or other pills
Flashes/highlights briefly to signal completion; vanishes; blocks above cascade downward
Disappears when surrounded by same-colored pill matches; visual feedback confirms elimination

notes: Tetris & Dr. Mario is a non-narrative puzzle compilation where world-state and danger are communicated purely through mechanical and visual manifestation of game rules. There are no factions in traditional sense (no opposing AI agents or political entities), but the two games function as distinct 'worlds' with different visual identities, rule systems, and threat models. Danger is purely systemic (filling playfield = loss condition). The Mixed Match mode serves as a meta-narrative device by forcing players to alternate between two contrasting puzzle paradigms, creating cognitive tension. Visual communication relies entirely on spatial occupancy, color, and object behavior rather than explicit UI elements or diegetic storytelling. The game uses color as primary information carrier in Dr. Mario (red/yellow/blue viruses and pills), while Tetris uses shape and spatial arrangement.
